currency: IDR billions (IDR T = IDR 1,000B shown separately for readability)
basis_note: FY2025 and prior are consolidated (including Bank Syariah Indonesia / BRIS). FY2026 onward is post-BSI deconsolidation (bank-only + non-BSI subsidiaries). BRIS deconsolidated effective January 23, 2026. BSI contributed approximately IDR 250-280T in loans, IDR 400T in assets, and IDR 4-5T in NPAT to the consolidated FY2025 figures. All FY2026+ paths are on the post-deconsolidation basis.
identity: NPAT = [(Loan Book x NIM) + Fee Income - (CIR x Total Operating Revenue) - (Credit Cost Rate x Loan Book)] x (1 - Tax Rate). Revenue is NII + Fee Income. NII = Earning Assets x NIM (simplified; earning assets approximated as loan book here). Tax rate 20%.

loan_book_anchors_idr_b.note: Post-BSI decon opening loan base estimated as FY2025 consolidated (1,895T) minus BSI loans (~275T) = ~1,620T. Then FY2026 loan growth of 8% applied to this base.

base.note: FY2025 consolidated revenue IDR 155.5T (NII IDR 106.8T + fee IDR 48.5T approx). FY2026 post-BSI base revenue is lower due to removal of BSI's sharia NII (~IDR 25-28T) partially offset by fee income growth. Revenue grows ~9% CAGR 2026-2028 as loan volume grows 8-9% and fee income grows 8-9%. NIM compresses from 4.89% to 4.60% (FY2026) then stabilises at 4.65% (FY2027-2028). The 2025-to-2026 apparent revenue step-down is a structural change from deconsolidation, not an operating deterioration.
sources: BMRI Q4 2025 earnings call (investing.com/news/transcripts/earnings-call-transcript-bank-mandiri-q4-2025): NIM guidance 4.5-4.7%, loan growth 7-9%
IDNFinancials Q1 2026 results: NIM 4.7%, loan growth +16% YoY post-BSI

bull.note: Bull: NIM holds at 4.75% (vs base 4.60%) as BI pauses, CASA improves. Loan growth 10% (vs base 8%). Fee income grows 13% YoY. Higher revenue base drives superior earnings leverage. NIM assumption: 4.75% FY2026, 4.80% FY2027-2028.
bull.ceiling_check: Bull NIM 4.80% is 90bps below BCA's 5.70% ceiling -- achievable if CASA ratio expands from ~72% to ~83%. Bull loan growth 11% is within BI's 8-12% system credit growth target. No ceiling breach detected.
sources: BCA 9M 2025 NIM 5.8% (kbvalbury.com/cfind/source/files/bbca-9m25-results-211025.pdf) -- establishes sector NIM ceiling

bear.note: Bear: NIM falls to 4.35% (25bps below guidance floor) as wholesale loan yields reprice faster than time deposits roll off. Loan growth 6% (below guidance, driven by SOE capex delays and macro caution). Fee income grows only 4% as digital growth plateaus and competition intensifies. BNI bear analog: NIM fell from 4.15% to 3.76% in 9M 2025 under similar wholesale-bank NIM compression dynamics.
sources: BNI 9M 2025 NIM 3.76% (bni.co.id/Portals/1/BNI/Perusahaan/HubunganInvestor/Docs/2025/BNI-Corporate-Presentation-3Q2025.pdf) -- wholesale state bank NIM compression precedent
Indonesia loan growth actual 7.77% OJK Jun 2025 (iru.ojk.go.id) -- floor reference for bear

base.note: Base NPAT: Q1 2026 annualized IDR 61.6T is the anchor (actual Q1 2026 IDR 15.4T x 4). CIR improves to 41% from 43.5%. Credit cost 65-70bps. NI margin ~46-47% on post-BSI revenue base is achievable given lower opex and lower provisions. FY2027 grows ~8% as NIM stabilises and volume expands. FY2028 normalization at ~9% growth. Phintraco Sekuritas forecasts IDR 55.6T FY2026 / IDR 59.7T FY2027 on a slightly more conservative NIM assumption; Theia base is ~10-11% above this, reflecting the stronger Q1 2026 momentum.
sources: Phintraco Sekuritas FY2026/FY2027 forecasts (search results compilation)
BMRI Q4 2025 guidance: CIR 40%, credit cost 60-80bps (investing.com Q4 2025 transcript)

bull.note: Bull: Higher NIM (4.75-4.80%) plus lower credit cost (55-60bps) plus stronger fee growth compresses CIR to 40-39.5%. NPAT grows 13-14% CAGR 2026-2028. Ceiling check: FY2028 bull NPAT IDR 86.9T requires equity ~IDR 387T and ROE 22.5%, which is below BCA's 24.1% ceiling -- feasible.
sources: Q1 2026 credit cost 58bps actual (IDNFinancials) -- bull credit cost is Q1 2026 run-rate sustained
BCA ROE 24.1% (9M 2025 actuals) -- establishes ceiling for comparable franchise ROE

bear.note: Bear: NIM falls to 4.35-4.30% and credit cost rises to 90-85bps (SOE event scenario). CIR deteriorates to 43-44% as revenue shortfall meets sticky opex. NPAT falls below FY2025 levels in FY2026 before recovering as credit cost normalizes in FY2028. Historical parallel: FY2021 NPAT IDR 28T (COVID) was the last major NPAT decline.
sources: BRI FY2025 NPL 2.15% / ROE 14.1% (idnfinancials.com/news/61682) -- MSME credit cycle precedent
BMRI FY2021 NPAT IDR 28T (databundle historicals) -- historical floor

base.note: Q1 2026 ROE was 22.1% on post-BSI basis (lower equity denominator). Full-year base assumes mean-reversion as credit cost normalizes to 65-70bps. Average equity grows ~5% YoY as NPAT is retained partially (payout ratio guided 65-70%). ROE of 19.5-20.5% is the honest most-likely range: not the optimistic steady-state-Q1 level, not the pessimistic fully-compressed scenario.
sources: FY2025 ROE 20.3% (Q4 2025 earnings call); Q1 2026 ROE 22.1% (IDNFinancials)
Dividend payout 65-70% FY2026 guidance (Q4 2025 earnings call)

bull.note: Bull ROE expansion driven by NIM resilience (4.75-4.80%), lower credit cost (55-60bps), and fee income acceleration. Average equity grows faster under bull due to higher retained earnings partially offset by higher dividends.
sources: BCA 24.1% ROE FY2025 -- establishes ceiling; bull assumes BMRI closes gap partially

bear.note: Bear ROE contraction from NIM trough (4.30-4.35%) and elevated credit cost (85-90bps). This is analogous to BNI's trajectory in 2025 (ROE compressed to 14.1%) but assumes BMRI's stronger franchise and coverage buffer maintains a ~16.5-17.5% floor rather than falling to 14%.
sources: BBNI FY2025 ROE ~14% -- wholesale state bank NIM compression bear analog
BMRI historical ROE minimum ~16% in 2015-2016 commodity downcycle

key_driver_ceiling: System-wide loans IDR 8,060T (OJK Jun 2025 +7.77% YoY). BMRI's loan book at IDR 1,620-1,750T (post-BSI) = ~20-22% of system. Bull FY2028 loan book of IDR 2,196T implies ~24-25% system share, which is plausible given BMRI's trajectory of outpacing system growth (13.4% vs 9.7% in FY2025). NIM ceiling: BCA at 5.70% (9M 2025) is the franchise ceiling for BUKU IV Indonesian banks. Bull NIM assumption of 4.80% is 90bps below BCA -- not a ceiling breach. ROE ceiling: BCA 24.1% FY2025 with superior CASA. Bull 22.5% is ~160bps below BCA -- structurally achievable for BMRI without franchise transformation. No ceiling breaches detected in any path.

bsi_deconsolidation_bridge.description: The FY2025-to-FY2026 revenue step-down in the base path (IDR 155.5T to IDR 130.1T) is structural, not operational. It reflects the removal of approximately IDR 25-28T in BSI sharia NII and IDR 3-4T in BSI fee income. The NPAT impact is smaller (BSI contributed only IDR 4-5T to consolidated NPAT) because BSI also had high opex. Post-deconsolidation, BMRI's NI margin and ROE are actually higher on a like-for-like basis because the equity denominator also fell by ~IDR 30-35T. Q1 2026 ROE of 22.1% confirms this mechanic.
